creator: pauamma@gundo.com
text:
(In reply to Mark LaPointe from comment #3)
"I will start off by stating that "read the man page" really isn't a proper line of thinking here. Otherwise there wouldn't be a need or desire for a handbook."

I agree with that. And I realize now that it wasn't clear to someone not familiar with what I work on for FreeBSD, but I didn't mean that the information not covered in the manual page (which would include the handholding you're right to ask about) doesn't need to be added *to* the manual page. I meant that knowing *what* is missing from the only existing information on the topic (the manual page) is a necessary precondition to deciding what and how to add to the Handbook.
